hypothesis: If training data for lattice structure properties are sampled either uniformly or with non-uniform experimentalist bias and are limited in size, then tensor completion surrogate models (CPD-S/NeAT and their ensemble) will achieve higher R² than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines when predicting Young’s modulus and mass-normalized Young’s modulus because they exploit the low-rank multi-way structure of the design–property tensor.
